content: Swift withTaskCancellationHandler + withCheckedContinuation bridge pattern: Task.cancel() alone does NOT terminate a subprocess or resume a withCheckedContinuation. Use withTaskCancellationHandler(operation: { await withCheckedContinuation { ... } }, onCancel: { /* terminate subprocess here */ }) to respond to task cancellation. The onCancel handler runs immediately when the task is cancelled, even if suspended. It must be @Sendable. Use a nonisolated(unsafe) var or shared box to pass the process PID from inside the continuation to the onCancel handler. The RunState.claim() pattern prevents double-resume since terminationHandler is still responsible for resuming the continuation.
